senses.gloss: fine hair (specifically from camels and goats, specifically from the hump; used for the cloths and tents of nomads from yore; distinguished from the مَدَر (madar, “loam”) of town buildings)

examples.text: ثُمَّ يُرْسِلُ اللّٰهُ مَطَرًا لَا يَكُنُّ مِنْهُ بَيْتُ مَدَرٍ وَلَا وَبَرٍ فَيَغْسِلُ الأَرْضَ حَتَّى يَتْرُكَهَا كَالزَّلَفَةِ
examples.english: Then God will send rain and there will be no brickhouse and no tent and he washes the earth, so that he leaves it blank like a nacre-shell.

senses.gloss: klipdachs, dassie, hyrax, daman (Hyracoidea, of which the rock hyrax Procavia capensis is found in the Arabian peninsula and Greater Syria)

etymology.text: From the root و ب ر (w b r) denoting a camel with a lot of hair or fur.
